title: Light fermionic dark matter in a scotogenic inverse seesaw model
authors: TODO: extract from paper front matter

models.name: scotogenic-inverse-seesaw
models.description: Scotogenic model with inverse seesaw neutrino mass generation. Dark matter is the lightest Z2-odd Majorana fermion chi1 from a 3x3 neutral fermion mass matrix.

references.form_factors_SI: Hoferichter, Ruiz de Elvira, Kubis, Meissner 2015 (Phys. Rev. Lett. 115, 092301)
references.xenon_abundances: Del Nobile, 'The Theory of Direct Dark Matter Detection' (2022, Springer Lect. Notes Phys. 996), Table B.1

notes: Paper uses NuFIT 6.0 (2024) with sin^2 values. Model parameters: m_E (vector-like doublet mass), m_N (Majorana singlet mass), y1, y2 (Yukawa couplings). Plan convention maps: M_R=m_E, mu_S=mu_1=mu_2=y*v/sqrt(2) (symmetric limit), M_N=m_N. BP masses: BP1 m_chi=42 GeV, BP2 m_chi=59 GeV, BP3 m_chi=61 GeV.
